'''Adam Sidlow''' (born 25 October 1987) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref name="rleague.com profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/s/sidlow_adam/index.php|title=rleague.com profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=2010-01-17|accessdate=2010-01-17|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20111130102850/http://rleague.com/db/player/s/sidlow_adam/index.php|archivedate=2011-11-30}}</ref>
He is assistant coach at [[St Helens RFC]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=17 Dec 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=St Helens appoint former Salford Red Devils forward in coaching role|url=https://www.totalrl.com/st-helens-appoint-former-salford-red-devils-forward-in-coaching-role/}}</ref>

He previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in two separate spells in [[RFL League 1|National League One]], [[Workington Town]] in [[2007 National League Two]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in [[2008 National League One]] and the [[Super League]]. Sidlow also played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in all three tiers of professional rugby league in Europe.

==Early career==
In his junior and youth career, he represented Lancashire Academy and was in the train-on squad for the [[England national rugby league team|England]] Academy.<ref name="Red Planet profile">{{cite web|url=http://reds.originalreddevils.com/squad/adam-sidlow|title=Red Planet profile|publisher=reds.originalreddevils.com|date=2010-01-17|accessdate=2010-01-17|archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100105074134/http://reds.originalreddevils.com/squad/adam-sidlow|archivedate= 5 January 2010 |url-status= live}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Salford City Reds===
[[File:Adam Sidlow.JPG|thumb|right|Sidlow playing for Salford]]
Sidlow played for Salford between 2008 and 2012.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sidlow-leaves-salford-for-bradford |title=Sidlow leaves Salford for Bradford |website=Love Rugby League |date=14 December 2012 |access-date=31 July 2024}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
'''2013''' - [[2013 Bradford Bulls season|2013 Season]]

Sidlow featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

He featured in fourteen consecutive games from round 1 [[Wakefield Trinity]] to round 14 against Leeds. He was injured for rounds 15-16 but he returned in round 17 [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] to round 21 [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Sidlow was injured for the rest of the season. Sidlow featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against the [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[London Broncos]].

'''2014''' - [[2014 Bradford Bulls season|2014 Season]]

Sidlow featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], Dewsbury and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

He featured in round 1 Castleford to round 4 against Hull FC. Sidlow also appeared in round 6 Hull Kingston Rovers and then round 8 [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] to round 9 Leeds. Sidlow played in round 11 [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] to round 26 [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]. Sidlow also featured in round 5 against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Challenge Cup]].

Sidlow re-signed with Bradford for another year even though they had been relegated to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

'''2015''' - [[2015 Bradford Bulls season|2015 Season]]

Sidlow featured in the pre-season friendlies against Castleford and Leeds

He featured in round 1 [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] to round 5 [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] then in round 7 [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] to round 22 against Leigh. Sidlow played in Qualifier 1 [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] to Qualifier 6 Leigh. Sidlow played in [[Million Pound Game]] against [[Wakefield Trinity]]. He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in round 4 [[Workington Town]] to round 5 against Hull Kingston Rovers.

'''2016''' - [[2016 Bradford Bulls season|2016 Season]]

He featured in round 1 [[Featherstone Rovers]] to round 5 [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] then in round 12 London Broncos to round 23 Featherstone. Sidlow played in the Championship Shield Game 1 [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] to the Final Sheffield.

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Following the Bulls' liquidation at the start of the 2017 season, Sidlow joined League 1 side [[Toronto Wolfpack]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.totalrl.com/one-two-toronto/|title=One in, two out at Toronto|date=13 January 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15022039.Sidlow_move_to_Toronto_confirmed_as_Bradford_hope_for_RFL_announcement_on_new_owner|title=Sidlow move to Toronto confirmed as Bradford hope for RFL announcement on new owner|work= [[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=13 January 2017|accessdate=20 February 2017}}</ref>

===Leigh===
On 20 November 2020 it was announced that Sidlow would join the [[Leigh Centurions]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=20 Nov 2020|accessdate=24 Dec 2020|title=Leigh bring in prop Adam Sidlow from Toronto Wolfpack|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-bring-in-prop-adam-sidlow-from-toronto-wolfpack/}}</ref>
In round 18 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for Leigh in a 28–34 loss against [[Hull KR]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58138787|title=Super League: Leigh Centurions 28-34 Hull Kingston Rovers - visitors edge 62-point thriller|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Sidlow played for Leigh in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
On 3 October 2022, Sidlow played for Leigh in their [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] which saw the club promoted back to the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12710793/leigh-44-12-batley-centurions-seal-super-league-return-with-deserved-win-in-championship-grand-final/|title=Leigh 44-12 Batley: Centurions seal Super League return with deserved win in Championship Grand Final|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
In the 2023 Super League season, Sidlow played 12 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Swinton Lions===
On 17 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Veteran prop sees League 1 move confirmed following Salford Red Devils exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/veteran-prop-sees-league-1-move-confirmed-following-salford-red-devils-exit}}</ref>

On 10 December 2025 he announced his retirement<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Dec 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League stalwart announces retirement as curtain comes down on 20-year career|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/adam-sidlow-retires}}</ref>
